body: PASS 19: fixed both blindspots with intent, not just flagged. WARRANT#32, SLS(47)/9.61, compiled clean. (1) Living-Edge state (day-of-week/triple-cell/regime-substrate) now persists across the ARMED-save instant-restart -- new guarded file section, same pattern as the existing ones, lives in the regular save path not the seal-on-pass-6 path (matching an established convention already in the code to keep that one tail-free). Before this, restarting from a save meant the inversion trim went quietly inactive until the next full calibration; now it survives. (2) Wired up the 'radioactive cell' concept from the mandate that never got built -- a cell whose phase just flipped (TRANSITIONING) now gets its own conviction trim regardless of which way the live signal points, stacking with the existing significant-inversion trim. Sent SLS(47).ex4 to mail -- it supersedes the SLS(46) I sent a bit ago, that one didn't have these two fixes yet. forge_ledger.json pass_19 filed, memory updated. Both fixes are code-reviewed and compile clean but still unverified against an actual run, same as everything else this session -- worth checking for the '[LOADED] Living-Edge state restored' line the first time a build restarts after a completed calibration.
